core_mechanism: assignment of an integer topological invariant (a winding number / Poincaré-Hopf index) to the isolated zeros of a gradient-type vector field derived from a scalar potential, classifying each critical point as stable or unstable and thereby characterizing the transitions between the potential's competing states
brief_reason: The method builds a generalized off-shell free-energy potential, treats its stationary points as the physical states, and computes a winding number that characterizes the phase transitions—i.e. a topological-charge count of the critical points of a potential field.
